prerequisites: No account, API key, or token is required: the TVmaze API is free and public for the schedule endpoints used here.
Network access to https://api.tvmaze.com so the connector can reach the public TVmaze REST API.
A domestic schedule country as an ISO 3166-1 code for the domestic_schedule_country_code field (for example US, or GB for the United Kingdom rather than UK).
A start_date in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) format for the start_date field; optionally an end_date in the same format to bound the range.
Awareness of the TVmaze rate limit of at least 20 calls every 10 seconds per IP address; requests beyond this may return HTTP 429.

authMethods.name: No authentication (public API)
authMethods.summary: The TVmaze schedule endpoints require no API key or token. This matches the connector, which defines no secret fields. Requests are made directly against the public TVmaze REST API with no credentials, using only the free public tier.

setupSteps.title: Confirm no credentials are needed
setupSteps.body: The TVmaze API is completely free and public for the schedule endpoints, with no API key or authentication required. The connector defines no secret fields, so there is nothing to provision or store. Just ensure outbound access to https://api.tvmaze.com.

setupSteps.title: Set the domestic schedule country and date range
setupSteps.body: Set domestic_schedule_country_code to the ISO 3166-1 country code whose traditional network schedule you want (for example US; use GB for the United Kingdom). This drives the /schedule endpoint. Set start_date (required) in ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD format, and optionally end_date (same format) to bound how far the sync reads.

setupSteps.title: Optionally configure the web (streaming) schedule country
setupSteps.body: Set web_schedule_country_code to pull the streaming/web schedule from the /schedule/web endpoint. Provide an ISO 3166-1 code to scope it to one country. Leaving it blank queries both local and global web channels, and setting it to an empty string retrieves only global web channels. Leave it unset to skip the web schedule.

setupSteps.title: Mind the rate limit
setupSteps.body: TVmaze allows at least 20 calls every 10 seconds per IP address and may return HTTP 429 when exceeded. Keep the configured date range reasonable and let the connector back off on 429 responses to stay within the limit.

sources.title: TVmaze API documentation (authentication, rate limits, schedule endpoints)
